column2: العلج: حار يابس يذهب بوخامة الطبيخ ويلطفه ويهيج الشهوة ويشحذها

column1: However, having too much of it will burn the blood, weaken eyesight, decrease sperm, invigorate black bile, and weaken yellow bile and blood.
column2: والإكثار منه يحرق الدم ويضعف البصر ويقلل المني ويقوي السودا ويضعف الصفر والدم

column1: It has a rarefying effect (yulattif) on the foods cooked with it.
column2: ويلطف الأطعمة إذا عملت به.

column1: Murri is similar to salt in its effects upon digestion. It is even stronger in properties |than salt] and more rarefying.
column2: المري: يعمل عمل الملح إلا أنه أقوى وألطف منه

column1: It is laxative and can break down viscidities and rarefy dense foods. It heats up the stomach and liver. Therefore, it induces thirst when sick people have it.
column2: يسهل البطن ويقطع اللزوجات ويلطف الأغذية الغليظة غير أنه يسخن المعدة والكبد ويعطش العليل

column1: It is extremely hot and dry and has the power to aid digestion with its strong properties.
column2: قوي الحرارة واليبس ويهضم الطعام بقوته.

column1: Coriander (kisfara) is cold and dry. It has the property to keep food in the stomach until it is completely digested.
column2: الكسفرة: باردة شانها أن توقف الغدا في المعدة ليتم هضمه

column1: Therefore, it should be taken sparingly if dense foods are eaten with it.
column2: ولذلك لا ينبغي أن يكثر منها في الأغذية الغليظة.

column1: Cumin (kammun} is hot and dry but less in degree than pepper. lt helps digest food and relieve bloating.
column2: الكمون: حار يابس دون الفلفل يهضم الطعام ويحلل النفخ.

column1: Caraway [karawya) is as hot and dry as cumin. However, it does not have adverse effects on the stomach. It also controls bowel movements.
column2: الكرويا: مثل الكمون في الحر واليبس غير أنه يوافق المعدة ويعقل البطن.

column1: Lovage (kasham) is hot and dry. It lingers in the stomach for a long while. Nevertheless, it eases bloating and arouses coitus.
column2: الكاشم: حار يابس غير أنه طويل الوقوف في المعدة ويحلل النفخ ويحرك الباه.

column1: Cassia [dar Sini) is hot and dry. It strengthens stomach and liver, repels gases, and aids digestion.
column2: الدار صيني: حار يابس مقوي للمعدة والكبد طارد للرياح معين على الهضم.

column1: Galangal (khulanjan) is hot and dry. It strengthens the stomach.
column2: الخولنجان: حار يابس مقوي للمعدة.

column1: Ginger (zanjabil) is even hotter [than galangal] and more effective in its digestive properties.
column2: الزنجبيل: أكثر حراً منها وأبعد انهضاما.

column1: Asafetida leaves [anjudhan] The black variety stinks [muntin). It is used medicinally only. The white variety is used in cooking. It is hot and dry.

column1: A recipe for making khall al-unsul (vinegar of squill):

Prepare basal al-fa‘r (squills), a knife and a large needle [mikhyat) both made of cane (qasab), and a woolen thread. Using the [cane] knife, peel off and discard the outer skins of the onions and take the middle parts. Discard any onions that are dry all the way to the core.
column2: تأخذ بصل الفار فتجعل له سكينا من قصب ومخيطا من قصب وخيط صوف ثم تقشر البصل بالسكين وتلقي خارجه ويؤخذ وسطه من أصوله ما كان حتى القلب

column1: When you have enough of these onions, thread them into the woolen string [using the cane needle] and hang them in a shaded place for three days until they shrink.
column2: فإذا جمعت منه حاجتك نظمته في الخيط الصوف ثم علقه في الظل ثلاثة أيام حتى يضمر

column1: Put 10 ratls (20 cups) of good quality sharp and sour grape vinegar in an earthen jar, big enough to be half-filled with it. Suspend the threaded onion on the vinegar. For each 5 qists (15 cups) (of vinegar) use 1 ratl (1 pound) onion. Do not let the hanging onions touch the vinegar, but rather let them be suspended above it by three fingers’ width (about 2 inches).
column2: ثم تأخذ من خل العنب الحامض الحاد الجيد عشرة أرطال فتجعله في جرة كبيرة تكون إلى نصفها وتعلق عليها البصل لكل خمسة أقساط رطل بصل ولا يماس البصل الخل بل يكون فوقه بثلاثة أصابع

column1: Seal the jar with mud and leave it out for 40 days under the sun of July when it is at its hottest.

column1: Then take out the onions and strain the vinegar. You may use this vinegar for making squill drink (sharab al-‘unsul).

column1: To store the vinegar, pour it into ajar, big enough to hold it. If you prefer it seasoned, add spikenard, cloves, and ginger.

column2: صفة خل الاشترغاز: يؤخذ الخل الأحمر من خل العنب أو الخمر فيصفى

column1: Take red vinegar made from grapes or wine and strain it.
For each 10 parts of vinegar add one part ishturghaz. Keep mixture in a container and seal it with mud until needed.

column1: Add wheat bran (nukhala) to vinegar and mash and press them in a sieve set on a bowl. Do this in small batches until you gather the amount you need [of the dripping liquid]. Add lime borax {buraq al-jir) and wait until it becomes clear [like water] without using an alembic or fire, God willing.

column1: A recipe for seasoned salt (milh mutayyab):
column2: صفة ملح مطيب:

column1: Take sumac, [dried] pomegranate seeds, asafetida root (mahrut), [dried] asafetida leaves (anjudhn), hulled sesame seeds, nigclla seeds (habbat al- sawda), hemp seeds {shahdanaj), flax seeds (bazr al-kattan), cumin, green terebinth berries (habbat al khadra).
column2: تأخذ سماقف وحب رمان ومحروت وانجدان وسمسا مقشورا وحبة السودا وشهد انج وبرز كتان وكمون وحبة الخضرا

column1: Toast each of these separately and add them to the salt while they are still hot, God willing.
column2: تلقي كل واحد ويطرح على الملح حارا إن شاء الله.

column1: Another recipe for delicious (tayyib) salt:
column2: ملح طيب آخر:

column1: Macerate for one day and one night [36 hours] [dried] sour pomegranate seeds in sour wine vinegar along with asafetida leaves (anjudhan).

column1: Then [strain the seeds,] toast them, pulverize them until very fine, and sift them. Add them to the salt along with thyme (sa'tar), toasted sesame seeds, toasted hemp seeds (shahdanaj), and pounded (dried) bread, God willing.
column2: ثم يلقى ويدق وينخل وشهدانج مقلو ثم يخلط معه خبز مدقوق إن شاء الله.

column1: A recipe for Khurasani salt:
column2: صفة ملح خراساني:

column1: Pound and sift good quality andarani salt. Put it in a tinjir (large copper pot; on slow-burning fire.
column2: يؤخذ الملح الدراني الجيد فيدق وينخل ثم يجعل في طنجير على نار لينة

column1: Put it away from heat and let it cool down.

column2: صفة ملح كان يعمل للرشيد:

column1: Take white pure salt and finely grind it.
column2: يؤخذ الملح الأبيض النقي فيطحن دقيقا

column1: Grind for it asafetida root (mahrut) and asafetida leaves (anjudhan) and stir them into the salt.

column2: ويلقى عليه مثل حبة السودا كما هي صحاح.

column1: A recipe for laxative salt (mushil):
column2: صفة ملح مسهل:

column1: It is good for gout (naqris), hemiphlegia (falaj) facial paralysis (laqwa), arthritis {awja al-mafasil), diseases caused by cold and humidity and spleen-related ailments. It also has a purging power [yajlu]
column2: ينفع من النقرس والفالج واللقوة وأوجاع المفاصل والأمراض التي تتولد من البرد والرطوبة وأوجاع الطحال ويجلوا.

column1: Take 6 uqiyyas (6 ounces) andarani salt, and 12 dirhams (12 ounces) black pepper Also take 4 dirhams (4 ounces) of each of the following: Zanjabil (ginger), dried zufa (hyssop), anjudhan (leaves of asafetida), bazr al-karafs (celery seeds), sadhaj Hindi (Indian leaf ghariqun (agaricum), saqmunya (scammony resin) hurf (seeds of garden cress), and qurturm (safflower seeds).

column1: Mix, grind, and sift all the ingredients. Store them in a glass jar (ina ’ Zujaj) and use as needed.

column2: ولإبراهيم بن المهدي في الملح:

column1: Following decrees of books of medicine, the ingredients I perfectly balanced.
column2: ومستخرج من حكمة الطب ومستخرج من حكمة الطب ألقيت حوايجه فيه فتم اعتداله به

column1: With it we begin our meals after the name of God we invoke, Exalting in His Glory.
column2: المبتدأ قبل الطعام وبعدما يسمى باسم الله جل جلاله

column1: Of spices, walnut, sesame, nigclla, asafetida root (mahrut), and carda¬mom (hal), Coriander toasted in tabaq, sumac and asafetida leaves {anjudhdn), equal parts.
column2: وفيه من الابزار جوز وسمسم وشونيز والمحروت فيه وهاله وكزبرة مقلوة فوق طابق وسماق جزء وانجدان مثاله

column1: And toasted hemp seeds [shahdanaj), thyme, and saffron pure rendered it complete.
column2: وشهدانج مقلو فيه وصعتر بالزعفران المحض تمت خصاله

column1: Know that salt left untoasted will not be savored. It's only by toasting and seasoning that it will be perfectly flavored.
column2: وماء الملح ما لم تقله تستلذه وبالقلي والتبزير تم كماله.

column1: HUMORAL PROPERTIES OF COLD DISHES
column2: الباب الثاني والعشرون
"في طبع الوارد من الطعام البارد

column1: Bazmaward (slices of rolled up sandwiches) are heavy and slow to digest. They putrefy the humors [kaymus).
column2: البزماورد: ثقيل على المعدة بعيد الانهضام ومعفن للكيموس

column1: The best time to eat them is at the beginning of the meal.
column2: وأصلح ما أكل في أول الطعام.

column1: Aswat (stuffed and pressed sandwiches) cause satiety. They are slow to digest especially if eaten at suppertime when they are more likely to cause surfeit {tukhama).
column2: الأوساط: متخمة بطيئة الهضم ولا سيما إذا أكلت بالعشي وربما أورثت التخم.

column1: Sanbusaj (fried filled pastries) are lighter than sandwiches (awsat) and faster to digest.
column2: السنبوسج: أخف من الأوساط وأسرع انهضاما.

column1: Fardrij (pullets) are good for people with hot temperaments if combined with juice of sour, unripe grapes and na'na' (cultivated mint).
column2: الفراريج: صالحة لأصحاب الحرارات إذا أكلت بماء الحصرم والنعنع.

column1: Firakh (young domestic fowls) are best eaten roasted, second best is having them braised (mutajjan) with oil and murri (liquid fermented sauce) without spices (abazir).
column2: الفراخ: أصلح ما أكلت شوي وبعده مطجنة بالزيت والمري بلا ابازير

column1: They are also very good eaten as mams (macerated and cooked in vinegar).
column2: واصلح ما أكلت مصوص

column1: They are good for people whose dominant humor is black bile (ashab al-sawda*).
column2: وهي صالحة لأصحاب السودا.

column1: Hajal (partridge). The best way to cook it is in vinegar (mauls) because it lowers its density and makes it beneficial to people with diarrhea.
column2: الحجل المصوص: لطيف إذا طبخ لأصحاب الاستطلاق والطف ما أكل مصوص

column1: The same rule applies to shafanin and sparrows (‘asafir).
column2: وكذلك الغانين "والعصافير.

column1: Cold dishes of roasted meat (shiwa barid) are less dense than hot dishes and much lighter.
column2: الشواء البارد: أقل غلظا من الحار وأمرأ

column1: They linger in the digestive system much longer and thereby are not good for people whose dominant humor is black bile.
column2: وهو بطي الاستمرار لا يصلح لأصحاب السودا

column1: If these cold dishes are eaten with tal,  they may generate black bile and curb coitus.
column2: وإذا أكل بالطلع أورث السودا وقطع الباه .

column1: These dishes are cold and bad for the stomach because they may dry it up. They help mollify the agitated blood.
column2: الطلع: بارد رديء للمعدة يجففها ويسكن ساير الدم.

column1: Boiled dishes (silaqa) are cold. However, serving them with mustard will make them more temperate.
column2: الصلاقة: باردة وإذا صب عليها الخردل اعتدلت

column1: They are good for people whose dominant humor is yellow bile but bad for those with black bile.
column2: صالحة لأصحاب الصفراء رديئة لأصحاب السودا.

column1: Cold sikbaj (beef stew soured with vinegar) is not good for raw chests. It extinguishes heat.
column2: السكباج الباردة: رديء لمكن خشونة في صدره مطفئ للحرارة

column1: It is not recommended for people with backache or those whose dominant humor is phlegm (ashab al- balgham).
column2: لا يصلح لمن به وجع الظهر ولا لأصحاب البلغم.

column1: Cold madira (white stew cooked with meat and sour milk) is less dense than when served hot.
column2: المضيرة الباردة: أقل غلظا من الحارة

column1: It suits people with extremely hot properties.
column2: وهي موافقة لأصحاب الحرارة المفرطة

column1: Cooking it with mastic will balance and improve its properties and make it delicious.
column2: سيما إذا ألقي فيها من المصطكي عدلها وأصلحها وطيبها

column1: Adding mustard foam to it will help expel phlegm.
column2: وأن ألقي فيها زبد الخردل هبت بالبلغم.

column1: Cold hisrimiyya (stew cooked with sour grape juice) is less dense (altaf) than hot hisrimiyya and more effective in suppressing yellow bile.
column2: الحصرمية الباردة: ألطف من الحارة وأقمع للصفرا.

column1: Cold adasiyya (lentil dish) is less dense {altaf) than when served hot.
column2: العدسية الباردة: ألطف من الحارة

column1: It is good for people who have had ‘ildj if eaten with a lot of oil and fresh onion.
column2: وتصلح لأصحاب العلاجات إذا أكلت بالزيت الكثير والبصل الرطب

column1: It helps expel phlegm and is particularly good for the chest. In this respect, tafshil (porridge of grains and legumes) has the same benefits.
column2: وأذهبت بالبلغم ونفعت الصدر منفعة بينة والطفثيل مثلها.

column1: Eggplant (badhinjan) is cold and extremely dry.
column2: الباذنجان: بارد يابس شديد اليبس

column1: Addiction to it engenders black bile.
column2: يولد السودا إذا أدمن أكله

column1: It burns the blood and causes mouth blisters, which can be prevented by cooking it with vinegar. When prepared this way, it will also extinguish yellow bile quite effectively.
column2: ويحرق الدم ويبثر الغم وإذا طبخ بالخل لم يبثر الغم وطفى الصفراء بقوة

column1: Eggplant can unclog any obstructions in the spleen.
column2: مفتح للسدد والطحال

column1: Buran is more harmful than other eggplant dishes and generates more black bile.
column2: والبوران أكثر ضررا وأجلب للسوداء

column1: Raw eggplant is the worst way to eat it. Grilling improves it a little and frying is somewhat better.
column2: وشر ما أكل الني ثم المشوي في النار ثم المقلو .

column1: Cold tabahija (braised red meat; is beneficial to people whose dominant humor is black bile.
column2: الطباهجة الباردة: شافعة لأصحاب السوداء.

column1: Gourd with yogurt (qar' bi 'l-lab an) is cold and moist and thereby is bad for the stomach and weakens the appetite.
column2: القرع باللبن: بارد رطب رديء للمعدة يسقط الشهوة

column1: However, it quenches thirst and extinguishes any heat in the stomach and liver.
column2: ويستلب العطش ويطفئ لهيب المعدة والكبد الحارين

column1: Chard roots (usul al-silq) and leaves are cold and bad for the stomach.
column2: أصول السلق: هي واللق باردين رديئين للمعدة

column1: They are laxative and flatulent but help break up and dissipate phlegm.
column2: مطلقين للبطن منفخين قاطعين للبلغم.

column1: Asparagus (hilyawn) is hot and moist.
column2: الهليون: حار رطب

column1: It invigorates coitus and warms up kidneys. It is not good for the stomach and may cause nausea.
column2: يزيد في الباء ويسخن الكلي وليس بجيد للمعدة وربما غثى.

column1: Truffle (kam'a) is cold and slow to digest. It is dense and generates raw humors (Kham). Red truffle is bad.
column2: الكماة: باردة بطية غليظة تولد الخام والحمراء منها رديئة

column1: Over-indulging causes colic [qawlanj). Truffles are best eaten seasoned with dry spices (tawabil).
column2: والإكثار منها يولد القولنج وينبغي أن توكل بالتوابل.

column1: Fresh beans (lubya ratba) are hot, moist, and low in density.
column2: اللوبيا الرطبة: حارة رطبة لطيفة

column1: They are good for thin people. Eating beans with vinegar benefits people whose dominant humor is yellow bile.
column2: صالحة لأصحاب الأبدان النحيفة وإذا أكلت بالخل نفعت أصحاب الصفراء.

column1: Cauliflower (qunnabit) is hot and dry. It increases blood and causes belly gurgles (qaraqir).
column2: القنبيط: حار يابس يزيد في الدم ويورث قراقر في البطن

column1: Its florets (bayd) are hard to digest and cause flatulence. Frying the vegetable is the best way to have it.
column2: وفي بيضه نفخ وهو عسر الانهضام وأخف ما أكل مقلات.

column1: Carrot (jazar) is hot and bloating and is not easy to digest. It is aphrodisiac and diuretic.
column2: الجزر: حار منفخ عسر الهضم بطي في المعدة يحرك الباء ويدر البول.

column1: Levantine leeks (kurrath Shami) is hot. To benefit the cold stomach and warm up the sperm and get it moving, boil leeks and serve them with vinegar, murri (liquid fermented sauce), and olive oil
column2: الكراث الشامي: حار إذا طبخ بالماء وأكل بالخل والمري والزيت نفع المعدة الباردة وسخن المني وحركه.

column1: Ahlam is dense (ghaliz) and putrefied (‘afin). It is bad for people whose dominant humor is black bile.
column2: الاهلام: غليظ عفن ردي لأصحاب السوداء

column1: Qaris (aspic) is good for people whose dominant humor is yellow bile.
column2: القريس: صالح لأصحاب الصفراء.

column1: MAKING SANDWICHES, ROLLED UP (BAZMAWARD) AND STUFFED {AWSAT)
column2: الباب الثالث والعشرون ذكر الاحتياط في عمل البزماورد والأوساط

column1: A recipe for making bazmaward (rolled up sandwiches), Baghdadi style:
column2: صفة بزماورد مديني:

column1: Prepare meat by pounding it the way you do with sausages (laqaniq).
column2: تأخذ لحم فتدقه كما تدق اللقانق

column1: Add a small amount of kidney fat, onion, fresh herbs {abzar rut), and rue.
column2: وتجعل فيه شيئا من شحم الكلي وبصل وابزار رطب وسذاب

column1: Add to these, coriander, black pepper, caraway, cumin, spikenard, cloves, nutmeg, ginger, and cassia, [all| pounded.
column2: ثم تلقي فيه كزبرة وفلفل وكرويا وكمون وسنبل وقرنفل وجوزبوا وزنجبيل ودار صيني مدقوق

column1: Break 5 eggs on the meat mixture and add a little chopped garlic and onion. Pound the mixture until it blends very well.
column2: ثم كسر عليه خمس بيضات وتقطع فيه شيئا من ثوم مدقوق وشيئا من بصل وتدقه حتى يختلط الجميع

column1: Spread caul fat [tharb] of sheep on a soft ruqaqa (thin sheet of bread). Let it cover as much as possible of the bread.
column2: ثم تأخذ شرب شاة فتبسطه على رقاقة لبق أكبر ما تقدر عليه

column1: Spread the pounded meat mixture on the caul fat.
column2: ثم تبسط فوقه ذلك اللحم المدقوق

column1: Take 5 boiled eggs, peel them but leave them whole. Arrange them in a row along the spread meat paste.
column2: ثم تأخذ خمس بيضات مصلوقة فتقشرها وتجعلها بالطول كل بيضة "كما هي صحيحة

column1: Lightly roll up the ruqaq bread with what is spread on it, and truss the roll with clean intestines.
column2: ثم تلف الرقاقة بما فيها لفا جيدا ثم تأخذ مصران نظيفا مهيأ فيلف على الرقاقة

column1: Put the roll on 4 sticks of khilaf tree (willow) and tie it with a thread so that it stays intact.
column2: ثم تجعل على أربع خشبات من عيدان الخلاف ويربط بخيط لئلا يفسد

column1: Lower it into the tannur and place it on a flat tile (ajur) put directly on the fire.
column2: وتدليه في التنور وتجعله على آجرة فوق النار

column1: When the roll is done, take it out of the oven and slice it crosswise into bazmaivard (slices).
column2: فإذا نضج أخرجه فقطعة بازماورد

column1: Arrange the pieces on a platter {jam) and serve them with Nabatean murri (liquid fermented sauce) or mustard, God willing.
column2: وصففه وقربه مع مري نبطي أو خردل إن شاء الله

column1: A recipe for bazmaward with citron pulp (hummad), called al-Ma'muni:
column2: عمل بزماورد بحماض يعرف بالماموني:

column1: Chop [cooked chicken] and spread it on ruqaq (thin sheet of bread).
column2: تقطع دجاجة وتصف على الرقاق

column1: Iet there be underneath the chicken some skinned walnut, citron pulp (hummad al-utruj), na'n' (cultivated mint), tarragon, batharuj (basil), and salt. Roll up the bread [with the filling inside].
column2: وتبسط تحت الدجاجة جوز مقشور وحماض الاترج ونعنع وطرخون وباذروج وملح وبدرج .

column1: Another recipe made for al-Mutawakkil:
column2: آخر عمل للمتوكل:

column1: Use cold [cooked | meat of two legs and shoulders of a kid or lamb. Finely shred the meat into thread-like pieces.
column2: يؤخذ من لحم جدي بارد وخروف بارد من الفخذين والمتن مما ينتف مثل الخيوط

column1: Choose whatever you like of leaf vegetables, excluding watercress (jirjir) and endives (hindiba').
column2: ثم تعمد إلى ساير أنواع "البقل غير الجرحير والهنديا

column1: Finely chop them until they resemble sesame seeds and mix [part of] them with the shredded meat. Set mixture aside.
column2: فتقطعه على مقدار السمسم وتخلطه مع اللحم المنتف ثم تعزله ناحية

column1: Now choose good quality sharp cheese, scrape it with a knife, and collect the scraped cheese.
column2: وتعمد إلى الجبن الحريف الجيد فتحكه بالسكين وتجمع ما سقط منه

column1: Coarsely grind walnut and add it [with the cheese] to the [set-aside meatless] chopped vegetables. Add as well some chopped herbs (abzar rutb) and rue.
column2: وتدق الجوز دقا جريشا وتخلط مع البقل والجبن وتقطع مع ابزار رطب وسذاب

column1: A portion of the chopped vegetables should have been set aside unmixed with meat.
column2: وليكن عندك من البقل المقطع معزول بلا لحم

column1: Now peel and chop some olives and add them to the [meatless] chopped vegetable mixture.
column2: ثم تعمد إلى الزيتون فتفرشه وتقطعه صغارا وتخلط معه

column1: Spread a soft (labiq) and large ruqaqa (thin bread), spread it with some of the meatless vegetable mixture and sprinkle it with seasoned salt (milh mutayyab).
column2: ثم تبسط رقاقة لبق كبيرة وتنثر عليها من البقل الذي ليس فيه لحم وتنشر عليه من الملح المطيب

column1: Next, spread the meat and vegetable mixture [to which you should have added] some hawayij.
column2: ثم تبط اللحم الذي مع البقل والحوايج فوق البقل الأول

column1: Then arrange a layer of eggs sliced lengthwise.
column2: وتصف عليه البيض المسلوق طولا حتى تمتلئ الرقاقة

column1: Next, spread another layer of the meat and vegetable mixture followed by a layer of the meatless vegetable mixture.
column2: ثم تنشر فوق البيض "من البقل الذي فيه اللحم ومن فوقه البقل الذي ليس فيه لحم

column1: Sprinkle them with pleasant-lasting salt (milh tayyib) and drizzle them with sweet vinegar (khall ‘adhb) and rose water.
column2: وتذر عليه ملحا طيبا وترش عليه شيئا من خل عذب وماء ورد

column1: Tightly roll the ruqaqa bread with the filling and slice it crosswise into rounds.
column2: وتلف الرقاقة بما فيها لفا جيدا وتقطع تقطعها مدورا

column1: Arrange the pieces on a platter (jam) and pass them around (tufarriquha), God willing.
column2: وتضعها في جام وتقرقها إن شاء الله.

column1: A recipe for wsat (filled sandwich) made by Ibn Dihqana:
column2: صفة وسط لابن دهقانة:

column1: Choose a round and thick bread made with fine, bran-free flour (raghif samik huwwara). Using a knife, cut it crosswise in half and set it aside.
column2: يؤخذ رغيف سمك حواري فيقشر قشرين بسكين ويعزل

column1: Take meat of cooled sikbaj (beef stew soured with vinegar) and shred it. Finely chop leaf vegetables (baql).
column2: ثم يؤخذ من لحم سكباج مبرد فينتف وتقطع البقل عليه صغارا

column1: Cover the [cut side of] one piece of the bread with the [chopped) vegetables followed by another layer of the prepared meat.
column2: ثم تعمد إلى البقل فتصفه على قشرات الرغيف الواحد وتصف اللحم من فوق البقل

column1: Sprinkle the surface with pleasant-lasting salt followed by chopped cheese, chopped olives, and [chopped] walnuts, almonds, pistachio, and pine nuts.
column2: وتنثر عليه ملحا طيبا وجبن مقطعا وزيتونا مقطعا وجوزا ولوزا وفستقا وصنوبرا

column1: Put another layer of the chopped vegetables and cover [the filling) with the other half of the bread [cut side down].
column2: ثم ابسط عليه البقل من فوق وأطبق القشر عليه

column1: Keep the prepared sandwich pressed [using a weight] for about an hour. Make as many as you wish of these sandwiches following the same rule.
column2: واكبه ساعة واعمل من "هذه الأوساط ما أحببت من قليل أو كثير على هذا العمل

column1: Cut the filled sandwiches into squares and then cut them into triangles (shawabir).
column2: ثم تقطع شوابير مربعا

column1: Arrange the pieces on a platter {jam) and serve them, God willing.
column2: وصفه في جام وقربه إن شاء الله.

column1: A recipe for open-faced sandwich (wast mashtur) made by Ibrahim bin al-Mahdi:
column2: صفة وسط مشطور لإبراهيم بن المهدي:

column1: Remove the top crust and edges of a whole bread (raghif).
column2: يؤخذ رغيف فيقشر جفاؤه وحروفه

column1: Spread (yushattar) its face with binn al-sakarij, slather it with walnut oil, and put it on a brazier (kanun) until it is thoroughly heated. Spread yolk of soft-cooked eggs (nimbarisht) on top of the binn and eat it.
column2: ويشطر بين السكارج ويطلى بدهن الجوز من فوقه ويلقى على الكانون فإذا ابيض فوق اللبن محاح البيض النيمبرشت وأكل

column1: You may sprinkle it with pounded cheese [jubn madquq), which will make it even more delicious.
column2: ومن أراد أن يذر عليه جبنا مدقوقا فعل فإنه طيب.

column1: Ibrahim bin al-Mahdi has a poem describing this sandwich:
column2: ولإبراهيم بن المهدي فيه :

column1: What a delicious sandwich on the brazier I made, slathered with binn and walnut oil.
column2: أطيب بوسط على الكانون صنعته ........ به وبدهن الجوز مشطور

column1: Fragrant and shining, as if the binn I used with ambergris and musk embalmed.
column2: "كأنما البن فيه عنبر صنعت ........ فيها من المسك تلميع وتأثير

column1: Of nigella seeds I put what it needed, as for fennel, I did sprinkle some.
column2: فيه من الحبة السوداء حصته ........ والرازيانج فيه فهو مذرور

column1: Olive oil I made sure to add, for it gives a luseious coating and a saffroned glow.
column2: وخلطة الزيت فيه عند صنعته ........ ففيه زيته غمر وتغمير

column1: Smeared with egg yolk, with cheese sprinkled, like speckled embroidered silk.
column2: عليه من مح بيض فوقه جبن ........ كأنه مطرف يعلوه منشور

column1: As colorful as striped silk it looks,
column2: كأنه رقم خزفي تلونه ........ وفي روائحه مسك وكافور

column1: exuding musk and camphor.
The taste, luscious as pure honey, for the best aromatic spices I did use.
column2: والطعم كالشهد في الأفواه لذته ........ وخلطه من افواي العطر مشهور

column1: CHAPTER 24
column2: الباب الرابع والعشرون

column1: HUMORAL PROPERITIES OF CONDIMENTS (KAWAMIKH), OLIVES, PICKLES, AND BINN
column2: في طبع الكواميخ من كل فن والزياتين والمخللات والبن والتين

column1: Binn is hot and dry. Although it induces thirst, it has the benefit of purging phlegm from the stomach and curing bad breath {bakhar).
column2: البن: حار يابس معطش إلا أنه ينقي المعدة من البلغم وينفع من البخر.

column1: Sihnat  is hot and dry. It induces thirst and purges phlegm from the stomach.
column2: الصحناة: حارة يابسة معطشة تخلو البلغم من المعدة

column1: However, addiction to it spoils the blood and causes mange (jarab).
column2: وإدمانها يفسد الدم ويولد الجرب.

column1: Kamukh al-kubur (condiment of capers) is hot and dry. It is bad for the stomach. It induces thirst and weakens the body.
column2: كامخ الكبر: حار يابس ردي للمعدة معطش نهوك للبدن.

column1: Kamakh al-thum (condiment of garlic) is good for phlegmatic people and those suffering from ague fits [humma al-nafid).
column2: كامخ الثوم: جيد لصاحب البلغم والحمى النافض

column1: It also has the benefit of dissolving stones.
column2: ويذيب الحصاة.

column1: Kamakh al-marzanjush (condi¬ment of marjoram) is good for flatulence, coldness in the stomach, and heavy-headedness caused by colds and phlegm.

column2: المخلات كلها تكتسب من الخل فضل لطاقة.

column1: They are good for obstructions in the liver and spleen.

column2: البصل: المخلل لا يسخن ولا يبرد ولا يعطش ويبعث الشهوة.

column1: I have already mentioned what might cause food to spoil. I will discuss here what utensils to use for the best cooking results.
column2: سقطمنالنسخة

column1: Cooking clean and excellent dishes is only possible if the cook is proficient in handling utensils in an adequately-equipped kitchen. Otherwise, if utensils arc lacking, the cook, for instance, might have to use a tool used for a sweet dish to stir a sour one. Alternatively, he might cut onion with the meat knife or use big pots in cooking dishes usually prepared in small pots. In this case, some particles might cling to the sides of the big pot then burn and fall back into the pot, which would definitely ruin the whole dish. Similarly, due to the lack of big pots, he might cook the same dish in two or three smaller ones, which would surely spoil the food and exhaust the cook who has to tend to two or three pots at a time instead of one.

Therefore, I start by mentioning the essential cooking utensils for the benefit of cooks who like to keep their food clean and enable them to make appetizingly fragrant dishes. If the cook forgets any of these culinary basics, he can always consult this book and find the information he needs stated at the proper place.
Soapstone pots {qudur biram) are the best for meat dishes.
The best porridges {harayis) are made in tin-lined copper pots {qudur nuhds muannaka).
Earthenware pots {qudur fakhkhdr) and tin-lined pots are suitable for making judhdbat, and the best of tannuriyydt are cooked in soapstone pots.
Condensed puddings {halwa) are to be cooked in brass cauldrons, and beverages in soapstone cauldrons (tandjir biram).

The most suitable frying pans for zcildbiya (fried fritters) are brass ones and for frying fish, iron frying pans {maqali hadxd)? (llr) Soapstone frying pans (maqali biram) are to be used for narjisiyyat and flat omelets (‘ujaj mabsutat).6 Rounded soapstone pots (qudur biram mudawwarat) arc the best for stir-fried dishes (maqlawiyydt).
Of fruits let him have fresh dates, pomegranate, and citron (utruj) with the peel.
